leadText: This article is about the Key Item in the Generation V games. For the segment at the end of Japanese episodes of Pokémon the Series: Black & White, see Professor Oak's Pokémon Live Caster. The Xtransceiver (Japanese: ライブキャスター Live Caster) is a Key Item in Generation V. Introduced in Pokémon Black and White, it has also made appearances in the spin-off media. As stated by the player's mother, the "X" in Xtransceiver is pronounced as "cross," making the complete name "cross-transceiver."

sections.title: Effect
sections.text: Effect [ edit | edit source ] Pokémon Black and White [ edit | edit source ] The player obtains their Xtransceiver early in the game from their mom . In the context of the game, it is a powerful communication tool, and allows the user to contact other Trainers; however, the player can only use it to talk to other players. At various points in the plot, the player will receive instructions from other characters to advance the storyline. The player's mother, Bianca , Cheren , and Professor Juniper all call the player at various points in the game. Using the C-Gear 's wireless feature, the Xtransceiver can be used to communicate with other real-life players nearby. Up to four players can talk to each other using a radio-like system, where players record an audio message which transmits to the other players after some time. The audio's pitch can be tuned with a slider to be higher or lower prior to recording. If they have a Nintendo DSi , Nintendo DSi XL , Nintendo 3DS , or Nintendo 3DS XL , they also have the option to see each other with the console's built-in camera. Players can also draw over their video using the touchscreen using a pen tool or place stickers, and the color of the pen and stickers can be changed with a dropper tool. Players can pause their video to keep a still frame. Pokémon Black 2 and White 2 [ edit | edit source ] In Pokémon Black 2 and White 2 , the Xtransceiver takes on a function like the calling functions of the Pokégear and PokéNav , allowing the player to call other people in-game through the Xtransceiver. However, only plot-relevant characters, in addition to either Curtis or Yancy , can be registered, although the latter two cannot be called as freely as the others. The player's mom is registered by default. Cheren, Bianca, and Professor Juniper are registered after defeating the Aspertia Gym . Hugh is registered after sailing from Virbank City to Castelia City . Curtis or Yancy can be registered in Nimbasa City after reaching certain requirements. Players can interact with other real-life players via local wireless like in Black and White, but the Xtransceiver could also be used over the internet by selecting it at the Global Terminal in a Pokémon Center. It cannot be used over the internet if selected from the Bag . In addition to the audio and video features, two minigames can be played: Balloon Catch and Balloon Ka-Boom! Both games involve balloons that display faces. If the player uses the Nintendo DSi, Nintendo DSi XL, Nintendo 3DS, or Nintendo 3DS XL, the player's actual face can be used instead of the default images. These games can only be played via local wireless. In Balloon Catch, players must catch their balloons as they float upward on the touchscreen. Players begin by taking three pictures of their face; one of a neutral expression, one of a smile, and one of a "troubled" look. Players will then have 60 seconds to tap as many balloons displaying their own face as they can. They will earn two points for tapping any of their balloons, and lose one point by tapping a balloon displaying their opponent's face. In the last 20 seconds of the game, balloons will appear faster and more frequently on the touchscreen, and points to be earned are doubled. All balloons show neutral faces at first. When a player taps their balloon, their smiling face will be shown. When tapping an opponent's balloon, the troubled look will be displayed. The player with the highest point total will win. If there is a tie, multiple players can be declared the victor. In Balloon Ka-Boom!, players pump balloons by alternately tapping the bottom corners of the screen until they pop. To earn the most points, they must pop as many balloons as possible. Players begin by taking photos of their face showing a neutral expression, a smile, and a "surprised" look. Each balloon will display the photos taken by the player, then those taken by their opponents after the first one is popped. As the balloons become inflated, their expressions will change in the order the photos were taken. Players are able to see their opponents' balloons behind theirs. In the first 40 seconds of the game, it takes 30 taps to pop a balloon. During the Final Sprint, which takes place during the remaining 20 seconds, it only takes 18 taps to pop each balloon. Players will earn five points each time they pop a balloon. The player with the highest point total will win. In the event of a tie, multiple players can be declared the victor.

sections.title: Description
sections.text: Description [ edit | edit source ] Games Description B W B2 W2 X Y OR AS S M US UM P E Sw Sh BD SP LA S V A high-tech transceiver with a camera function. It allows up to four-way calls.

Acquisition [ edit | edit source ] Games Method B W Nuvema Town (received from Mom at the start of the game) B2 W2 Aspertia City (in the player 's Bag at the start of the game)

sections.title: Trivia
sections.text: Trivia [ edit | edit source ] In Pokémon Black and White , Xtransceivers are blue for males and pink for females. In Pokémon Black 2 and White 2 , they are red for males and yellow for females. Hugh has a light purple Xtransceiver. The watchband of the Xtransceiver is black except for Bianca and Rosa , whose watchbands are white. In Pokémon Black and White, when prefixed with the indefinite article , this item is referred to as "an Xtransceiver". In Pokémon Black 2 and White 2, these instances are corrected to "a Xtransceiver", matching the pronunciation given in-game. In Pokémon Black 2 and White 2, the player cannot use the calling function while aboard the Plasma Frigate , and characters who are on the ship cannot be called from outside. The Holo Caster from Pokémon X and Y works similarly to this item and has a similar Japanese name.
